spacing.$description: Semantic spacing tokens for the River Romney design system. Maps intent-named roles to primitive space values. Use these in UI code rather than raw primitives — they communicate purpose, not just size. Scale runs from micro-spacing within a component to full-bleed section breaks.

micro.$description: Micro spacing. For icon-to-text margins, gap between a badge and its label, and tight internal nudges within a single element. At 4px the gap signals that two things are part of the same unit — barely breathing room, not real separation. Resolves to: 4px.

tight.$description: Tight spacing. Between closely related items that belong to the same thought — e.g., an icon and its label, a checkbox and its text, stacked lines within a single element. At 8px, the gap is felt as belonging rather than separating. Resolves to: 8px.

inline.$description: Inline spacing. Between items that are related but distinct — e.g., items in a navigation row, pills in a tag group, or gaps in a horizontal list. Signals adjacency without implying the items are the same unit. Resolves to: 12px.

element.$description: Element spacing. The default internal padding for most components — input fields, buttons, menu items, list rows. Sets the baseline breathing room inside a discrete UI unit. Also used between sibling elements within the same component zone. Resolves to: 16px.

component.$description: Component spacing. Between distinct UI components in a layout — e.g., the gap between a label and its input, between a card and the next card in a list, or between a toolbar and the content below it. Signals that two things are related but not the same. Resolves to: 24px.

group.$description: Group spacing. Between related groups of components within a layout zone — stacked form sections, card grid gaps, gap between a heading block and its content cluster. Fills the jump between component (24px) and layout (48px). Resolves to: 32px.

layout.$description: Layout spacing. Between major layout regions within a page section — e.g., between a sidebar and main content, between a page header and the first content block, or between stacked content groups. Larger than component spacing but not a full section break. Resolves to: 48px.

block.$description: Block spacing. Between major content blocks within a page section — distinct from component groupings but not a full section break. Use for the vertical gap between a text block and a media element, between a heading cluster and a feature grid, or between stacked editorial rows. Resolves to: 64px.

page.$description: Page spacing. Between major editorial sections — the standard vertical separator for hero-to-content and section-to-section gaps in portfolio and documentation contexts. Larger than spacing.block (content-level rhythm) but not as expansive as spacing.section (full page breathing room). Resolves to: 80px.

section.$description: Section spacing. Between top-level page sections — the full whitespace beat that tells the eye a new chapter has begun. Use for gaps between hero, features, testimonials, and footer regions. Not for component-level gaps; it will feel like the page fell apart. Resolves to: 128px.
